name: Israel
overview: Israel's Protection of Privacy Law, 5741-1981 — one of the world's older privacy statutes — was dramatically modernised by Amendment No. 13, which took effect on 14 August 2025. The amendment redefines core terms toward GDPR concepts, slashes database-registration duties while adding notification duties, mandates privacy protection officers for large processors, and arms the Privacy Protection Authority with substantial administrative fines. Israel retains EU adequacy (reaffirmed 2024).

legal_instruments.title: Protection of Privacy Law, 5741-1981 (as amended through Amendment No. 13)
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law covering databases of personal data: modernised definitions (personal data, sensitive data, processing), reduced registration but new PPA notification duties, DPO requirements for major data holders, and a new administrative enforcement regime with significant fines.

legal_instruments.title: Protection of Privacy Regulations (Data Security), 5777-2017
legal_instruments.summary: Tiered data-security requirements by database sensitivity, including mandatory breach notification to the PPA for severe incidents and periodic risk assessments.

legal_instruments.title: Privacy Protection (Transfer of Data to Databases Abroad) Regulations, 5761-2001
legal_instruments.summary: Conditions for transfers abroad: destination law comparable to Israeli law (EEA effectively adequate), consent, contractual safeguards or other listed grounds.

developments.title: Amendment No. 13 takes effect
developments.summary: The most significant reform since 1996 became operative: GDPR-style definitions, PPA administrative fines (potentially reaching millions of shekels for large databases), expanded investigative powers and DPO mandates.

developments.title: EU reaffirms Israel's adequacy status
developments.summary: The European Commission's review of the 2011 adequacy decision concluded Israel continues to provide adequate protection, noting the reform trajectory.

penalties: Post-Amendment 13: administrative fines scaled to violation type and database size (for large-scale sensitive databases, fines can reach NIS millions); criminal offences and statutory damages without proof of harm (up to ~NIS 50,000) in civil suits also available.
cross_border: 2001 Transfer Regulations: transfer permitted where destination ensures comparable protection (EEA listed), or with consent, contractual undertakings, or other enumerated grounds.
